title: [LV] Fix miscompile with conditional scalar assignment + tail folding
author: MacDue
description:
Previously, we could miscompile when vectorizing conditional scalar assignments with forced tail folding, as the backedge select could be based on the header mask, not the assignment conditional.

This resulted in a number of failures in the LLVM test suite when building with `-O3 -march=armv8-a+sve -mllvm -prefer-predicate-over-epilogue=predicate-dont-vectorize`.

The patch reworks `handleFindLastReductions()` to correctly handle tail folding.

tests.test_body:
target triple = "aarch64-linux-gnu"

define i32 @simple_find_last_reduction(i64 %N, ptr %data, i32 %a) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %data.phi = phi i32 [ -1, %entry ], [ %select.data, %loop ]
  %ld.addr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %data, i64 %iv
  %ld = load i32, ptr %ld.addr, align 4
  %select.cmp = icmp slt i32 %a, %ld
  %select.data = select i1 %select.cmp, i32 %ld, i32 %data.phi
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit.cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %exit.cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %select.data
}

target triple = "aarch64-linux-gnu"

define i32 @non_speculatable_find_last_reduction(ptr noalias %a, ptr noalias %b, i32 %default_val, i64 %N, i32 %threshold) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %latch ]
  %data.phi = phi i32 [ %default_val, %entry ], [ %select.data, %latch ]
  %a.addr = getelementptr inbounds nuw i32, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %ld.a = load i32, ptr %a.addr, align 4
  %if.cond = icmp sgt i32 %ld.a, %threshold
  br i1 %if.cond, label %if.then, label %latch

if.then:                                          ; preds = %loop
  %b.addr = getelementptr inbounds nuw i32, ptr %b, i64 %iv
  %ld.b = load i32, ptr %b.addr, align 4
  br label %latch

latch:                                            ; preds = %if.then, %loop
  %select.data = phi i32 [ %ld.b, %if.then ], [ %data.phi, %loop ]
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit.cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %exit.cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %latch
  ret i32 %select.data
}

define i32 @simple_csa_int_select(i64 %N, ptr %data, i32 %a) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %data.phi = phi i32 [ -1, %entry ], [ %select.data, %loop ]
  %ld.addr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %data, i64 %iv
  %ld = load i32, ptr %ld.addr, align 4
  %select.cmp = icmp slt i32 %a, %ld
  %select.data = select i1 %select.cmp, i32 %ld, i32 %data.phi
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit.cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %exit.cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %select.data
}

define i32 @simple_csa_int_load(ptr noalias %a, ptr noalias %b, i32 %default_val, i64 %N, i32 %threshold) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %latch ]
  %data.phi = phi i32 [ %default_val, %entry ], [ %select.data, %latch ]
  %a.addr = getelementptr inbounds nuw i32, ptr %a, i64 %iv
  %ld.a = load i32, ptr %a.addr, align 4
  %if.cond = icmp sgt i32 %ld.a, %threshold
  br i1 %if.cond, label %if.then, label %latch

if.then:                                          ; preds = %loop
  %b.addr = getelementptr inbounds nuw i32, ptr %b, i64 %iv
  %ld.b = load i32, ptr %b.addr, align 4
  br label %latch

latch:                                            ; preds = %if.then, %loop
  %select.data = phi i32 [ %ld.b, %if.then ], [ %data.phi, %loop ]
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit.cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %exit.cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %latch
  ret i32 %select.data
}

define i32 @find_last_trunc_iv(ptr %src, i64 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %rdx.next, %loop ]
  %gep.src = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %src, i64 %iv
  %l = load i32, ptr %gep.src, align 4
  %cmp103 = icmp eq i32 %l, 0
  %0 = trunc i64 %iv to i32
  %rdx.next = select i1 %cmp103, i32 %0, i32 %rdx
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %ec = icmp eq i64 %iv, %n
  br i1 %ec, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %rdx.next
}

comments.author: MacDue
comments.body:
> > -O3 -march=armv8-a+sve -mllvm -prefer-predicate-over-epilogue=predicate-dont-vectorize.
> 
> Would probably be good if one of the SVE bots could also run test-suite with that config

A short-term fix may be to force tail-folding for some CSA tests in the test-suite. Can the something like the `vectorize_predicate(enable)`  pragma do that?
